seo.title: Building Research Agents with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programming LM-based Research Agents through composable, optimizable modules for multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis. Guardrails are essential to prevent overfitting prompts, faulty chain-of-thought reasoning, and uncaught assertion failures that can produce misleading summaries. Vex integration ensures source verification, claim accuracy, recency, and bias detection for reliable outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
DSPy Research Agents typically follow a multi-module pipeline: a SearchModule retrieves relevant web sources, a RetrieveModule extracts key claims, an AnalyzeModule performs chain-of-thought reasoning on evidence, and a SynthesizeModule generates final summaries. Modules are connected via signatures defining input/output formats, with DSPy's optimizer tuning prompts and weights across the pipeline. This modular approach allows end-to-end optimization but introduces risks in module composition and reasoning chains.

The agent iterates through search-refine-synthesize cycles, using assertions to validate intermediate outputs. Common patterns include ChainOfThought signatures for analysis and custom Retrieve modules wrapping search APIs. Vex verification wraps critical forward() calls to catch failures before propagation.
architectureOverview.diagram:
SearchModule --> RetrieveModule --> AnalyzeModule --> SynthesizeModule
   |                  |                 |                 |
   +--[vex.verify()]--+--[vex.verify()]--+--[vex.verify()]--+--[Final Summary]
   |                  |                 |
Web Sources    Claims            Reasoning         Assertions

risks.name: Optimized prompts overfitting to training examples
risks.description: DSPy optimization creates prompts too specific to synthetic training data, failing on real web queries
risks.mitigation: Wrap signature calls with Vex source verification and claim checks against live web data

risks.name: Chain-of-thought reasoning producing plausible but wrong logic
risks.description: AnalyzeModule generates convincing but factually incorrect reasoning chains
risks.mitigation: Verify reasoning steps with Vex claim accuracy and recency assertions

risks.name: Assertion violations not caught at inference time
risks.description: DSPy assertions fail silently during optimized inference, propagating bad data
risks.mitigation: Use vex.verify() on module.forward() to enforce runtime assertions

risks.name: Module composition creating unexpected output distributions
risks.description: Search+Analyze pipeline produces hallucinated sources or biased summaries
risks.mitigation: Apply Vex bias detection and source verification across module boundaries

implementation.title: Multi-step Optimization Setup
implementation.description: Configure DSPy optimizer for end-to-end research pipeline
implementation.code:
from dspy.teleprompt import BootstrapFewShot

# Define trainset with research examples
trainset = [
    dspy.Example(query='AI safety trends', sources=['...'], claims=['...']).with_inputs('query')
]

agent = research_agent()
optimizer = BootstrapFewShot(metric=research_metric)
optimized_agent = optimizer.compile(agent, trainset=trainset)

implementation.title: Synthesis Module with Assertions
implementation.description: Final synthesis module with DSPy assertions
implementation.code:
class SynthesizeSignature(dspy.Signature):
    '''Synthesize unbiased summary from verified claims.'''
    claims: list[str] = dspy.InputField()
    sources: list[str] = dspy.InputField()
    summary: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='Concise, factual summary')
    confidence: float = dspy.OutputField(desc='0-1 confidence score')

class Synthesizer(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.synthesize = dspy.ChainOfThought(SynthesizeSignature)

    def forward(self, claims, sources):
        pred = self.synthesize(claims=claims, sources=sources)
        # DSPy assertion
        dspy.assertions.assert_confidence(pred, min_confidence=0.7), \
            'Low confidence synthesis requires human review'
        return pred

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Module Calls with vex.verify()
vexIntegration.description: Protect core module forward() calls with comprehensive verification
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from research_agent import research_agent

@vex.verify([
    vex.check.source_verification(min_sources=3, freshness_days=30),
    vex.check.claim_accuracy(threshold=0.8),
    vex.check.recency(days=90),
    vex.check.bias_detection(max_bias_score=0.3)
])
def verified_research(query: str) -> dict:
    agent = research_agent()
    result = agent.forward(query)  # Wraps the optimized forward call
    return {
        'query': query,
        'sources': result.sources,
        'claims': result.claims,
        'verified': True
    }
